evidence: Method names like buildRawCollocateProfiles, fetchSeedCollocates, identifyCoreCollocates, buildCollocateReversePattern, buildNounToCollocatesMap are precise and phase-labelled — a developer immediately understands which algorithmic step each method owns.
Class names SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator, SemanticFieldExplorer each name the algorithm variant they implement, not just a generic 'service' or 'handler'.

Identical types RelationEdgeType and Edge exist in both pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.viz and pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model.exploration — same name, same shape, different package — creating vocabulary duplication and forcing callers to import-qualify or cast.

nounCqlConstraint (SingleSeedExplorer field) vs collocatePattern (CollocateProfileComparator field): both hold CQL pattern strings but use different suffixes ('constraint' vs 'pattern'), diverging from the codebase's prevalent 'pattern' vocabulary.

Variable wordSketchResult is used as a loop variable in MultiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection and buildDiscoveredNouns even though those methods belong to the exploration layer, not the word-sketch layer — bleeds a foreign concept into the variable name.

collocs abbreviation in MultiSeedExplorer.buildDiscoveredNouns (line 141: 'List<...> collocs = seedCollocateMap.getOrDefault(seed, List.of())') is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase, which uses collocates or collocateScores in full.
SEED_COLLOCATE("seed_adj") in RelationEdgeType: the enum constant says 'collocate' (general) but the JSON label says 'seed_adj' (adjective-specific), a mild name/serialization mismatch.

data local variable in MultiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection (SeedCollocateData data = fetchCollocatesPerSeed(...)) is generic — a developer reading the method body must check the type declaration to understand what 'data' holds.

strengths: Algorithm method names label their phase explicitly: buildRawCollocateProfiles, fetchSeedCollocates, identifyCoreCollocates, filterByMinShared — each tells the developer exactly which step of the algorithm it owns.

Class hierarchy names are non-generic: SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator all name the specific algorithm variant they implement rather than using vague Handler/Manager/Service suffixes.
Domain vocabulary (collocate, logDice, seed, relation, lemma) is used consistently and precisely across all layers — the ubiquitous language is well-enforced.

Private helpers like prefetchCorpusFrequencies, buildCollocateFrequencyMap, performCollocateSearch are named to distinguish purpose from their callers (executeCollocateSearch vs performCollocateSearch vs executeCollocations).
Constant names like FALLBACK_COLLOCATE_PATTERN and FALLBACK_NOUN_PATTERN are distinguished rather than merged into a single vague DEFAULT_PATTERN.
naming_quality.issue_character: Issues are isolated rather than systemic: one vocabulary-duplication problem with the duplicate Edge/RelationEdgeType types across packages, a few local variable names that are abbreviated or import a foreign concept, and a minor constraint/pattern suffix inconsistency — none of these reflect a global naming philosophy problem.
naming_quality.score_rationale: The codebase demonstrates strong naming discipline: precise method names, non-generic class names, and consistent domain vocabulary make it easy to navigate. The main drag comes from the duplicate RelationEdgeType and Edge types in two packages (same name, different package — a concrete confusion point for any developer adding edges), the nounCqlConstraint vs collocatePattern inconsistency, and wordSketchResult used in exploration loops. These are real but localized defects in an otherwise well-named codebase, placing it solidly in the 'solid but uneven' band rather than exemplary.

issues.summary: Identical Edge and RelationEdgeType types exist in both viz and model/exploration packages, creating vocabulary duplication
evidence: viz/Edge.java and model/exploration/Edge.java are structurally identical records (source, target, weight, type).
viz/RelationEdgeType.java and model/exploration/RelationEdgeType.java are byte-for-byte identical enums with the same constants and labels.
Having two identically-named types for the same concept forces callers to import-qualify or choose one arbitrarily, and makes it unclear which is canonical.
issues.suggestion: Consolidate into a single canonical location — model/exploration is the better fit since it is the domain model layer. Remove the viz duplicates and update viz classes to import from model/exploration.

issues.summary: nounCqlConstraint and collocatePattern use divergent suffixes for the same concept (a CQL pattern string)
evidence: SingleSeedExplorer: private final String nounCqlConstraint — holds a CQL bracket expression such as [xpos="NN.*"].
CollocateProfileComparator: private final String collocatePattern — holds a CQL bracket expression such as [xpos="JJ.*"].
The rest of the codebase consistently uses the 'pattern' suffix (bcqlPattern, cqlPattern, simplePattern, collocateReversePattern).
issues.suggestion: Rename nounCqlConstraint to nounCqlPattern in SingleSeedExplorer to align with the codebase's prevalent 'pattern' suffix convention.

issues.summary: Loop variable wordSketchResult used in exploration code imports a word-sketch concept into an unrelated algorithm
evidence: The multi-seed exploration algorithm has nothing to do with word sketches; the variable name wordSketchResult is confusing because it implies the code is doing word-sketch work.
issues.suggestion: Rename loop variables to collocate or collocateResult to reflect the exploration context (e.g., 'for (QueryResults.WordSketchResult collocate : entry.getValue())').

issues.summary: collocs abbreviation in MultiSeedExplorer.buildDiscoveredNouns is inconsistent with the codebase's full-word convention
evidence: The rest of the codebase uses collocates, collocateScores, collocateProfiles, collocateFreqMap — never the truncated collocs form.
issues.suggestion: Rename collocs to collocates or seedCollocates for consistency with the codebase convention.

root_causes: The viz package appears to be a legacy or parallel layer that was not cleaned up when the model/exploration layer was introduced, leaving duplicate type definitions.
The constraint/pattern suffix divergence and collocs abbreviation suggest the affected classes were written at different times without cross-referencing the naming conventions established elsewhere.
